lemon_market_new_listings.description: The car is in fair condition. The exterior has visible scratches and minor dents, and the interior shows moderate wear. It is mechanically functional but requires some maintenance.
Poor condition.

This vehicle is in fair condition. The exterior has visible scratches and several small dents. The interior shows moderate wear and tear on the upholstery. The engine is operational but requires regular maintenance.

This vehicle is in absolute mint condition, appearing exactly as it did when it first left the showroom floor. It has been meticulously garaged and cared for, with the exterior paint maintaining a flawless, high-gloss finish entirely free of chips, scratches, or imperfections. The interior is equally pristine, showing no signs of wear on the leather upholstery or carpets, and it still retains that new-car scent. Every mechanical component has been maintained to the highest standard with a complete, documented service history included. A buyer can expect a car that feels and drives like new, offering a rare opportunity to own a perfectly preserved example of this model.

This vehicle is in truly mint condition, representing a rare find for any serious buyer. The exterior is absolutely flawless, with the original paint retaining its deep factory showroom luster and showing no chips, scratches, or imperfections of any kind. Inside, the cabin is immaculate and remains in like-new condition, with the upholstery and dashboard showing zero signs of wear. Mechanically, the car has been maintained to the highest possible standards with a complete service history, performing exactly as it did the day it was first delivered. This is an exceptional, collector-quality example that has been stored in a climate-controlled environment and meticulously cared for.

Offered is a mechanically sound E46 M3 featuring the high-revving S54B32 inline-six engine with its signature individual throttle bodies and double-VANOS variable valve timing. This example utilizes the Getrag 420G six-speed manual transmission, providing precise gear engagement and direct power delivery to the rear-mounted limited-slip differential. The chassis has been addressed with critical preventative maintenance, including rear subframe reinforcement and refreshed suspension bushings to maintain its legendary lateral grip. While the exterior shows minor cosmetic wear consistent with spirited use, the powertrain remains robust with a linear power band and an 8,000 RPM redline. A turn-key driver's car for the purist seeking telepathic steering feedback and a balanced 50/50 weight distribution.
